raw_codings.rationale: Names results (things chosen/selected); static and resultant in ordinary sense.
Word carries both constructive overtones (from 'fabricate'='make') and passive sense (the made thing); context-dependent.
Foregrounds the shaping/forming activity; 'volitional formations' emphasizes volitional activity constructing experience.
Technical term naming states or circumstances that obtain; colourless and passive.
Explicitly uses past-participle passive construction; emphasizes things that HAVE BEEN conditioned.
Ordinarily names ongoing sequences and unfolding activities; foregrounds the doing/activity aspect.
Ordinarily names results—ideas and thoughts conceived; passive sense of mental products.
Generic colourless term; does not inherently foreground making, activity, result, or conditioned status.
Emphasizes the volitional acts of deciding/settling/fixing; foregrounds active determination.
Foregrounds doing and acting; emphasizes the performative/active aspect in ordinary sense.
Inherently emphasizes the act/process of building/making despite naming results; 'construct' is fundamentally about the making.
Names the faculty or act of willing/choosing; foregrounds volitional force and active power.
Emphasizes active power, potency, and driving energy; inherently active sense.
Names results that are compounded together; passive sense of things that have been put together.
Foregrounds volitional agency and the act of choosing/selecting among options
Emphasizes the act of making or constructing; the verb 'fabricate' stresses the active manufacturing process
Names the result of forming—structures and configurations that have been formed
Names states or circumstances that have come to be; foregrounds the conditioned result
Explicitly identifies things that have been conditioned; directly passive framing
Generically names procedure or sequence without committing to active doing or passive result
Names mental contents that have been formed; foregrounds the result (idea/notion) rather than the act
Colourless, generic term that does not commit to active or passive framing
Foregrounds the act of determining/deciding; emphasizes active agency and will-setting
Explicitly names active doing and engagement; foregrounds dynamic ongoing process
Emphasizes the act of building and volitional shaping; the verbal root meaning dominates
Names acts of will and willing; foregrounds volitional agency and intention-setting
Emphasizes dynamic power and active causal/volitional agency; the driving force
Names things that have been compounded together; foregrounds the result of combining
Emphasizes volitional decision-making and the act of choosing rather than a static result.
Suggests something actively made or constructed; the verb 'fabricate' foregrounds the making/doing process.
Ambiguous between the act of forming and the result formed; neither pole is strongly foregrounded.
Names states or circumstances that obtain; emphasizes the conditioned result rather than the conditioning process.
The adjective 'conditioned' explicitly marks these as products or results that have been conditioned.
Foregrounds activity, unfolding, and dynamic doing rather than static phenomena.
Names conceptual structures already in place (results of conceiving) rather than the act of conceiving.
Completely generic term with no semantic lean toward construction or conditioning.
Carries the sense of determining, deciding, resolving—volitional activity and agency.
Explicitly foregrounds doing, action, and ongoing process rather than static results.
The noun is ambiguous between the building process and the built structures; neither pole dominates.
Names the will, intentional striving, and volitional acts—emphasizing active will-force.
Emphasizes power, dynamism, and active causal agency rather than static conditioned things.
Names the result of compounding—things made up of parts that have been put together.
Emphasizes the act of choosing, the volitional decision-making process and active selection
Foregrounds the act of making/fabricating, the volitional shaping and constructing activity
Can point either to the act of forming (active) or to that which is formed (passive); the word itself is colourless about direction
Emphasizes the state or thing—conditioned phenomena and results, not the act of conditioning
Explicitly foregrounds the result—things that have been conditioned, not the act of conditioning itself
Can mean either the act of processing or the pattern/thing that processes; treated generically without clear directional commitment
Mental contents and results of the mind's activity, not the active conceiving itself; treats them as things
Entirely non-committal, purely generic and colourless; names all things without foregrounding construction or conditioning
Foregrounds the act of determining, deciding, and shaping what will be; emphasizes the doing and the force that settles
Clearly foregrounds the act, the doing, and the active process; directly names volitional doing
Foregrounds the act of constructing, building, and shaping; primarily evokes the making process
Directly names the act of willing and the volitional force and activity; most explicitly active rendering
Foregrounds power and activity—the dynamic volitional doing that shapes experience; emphasizes active agency
Emphasizes the result—things that are put together and composite phenomena; foregrounds the state of being compounded
Emphasizes the act of choosing, volitional decision-making, and agency in selecting.
Carries inherent sense of making and fashioning; emphasizes the active process despite potential result-reading.
Deliberately ambiguous between the act of forming and the things formed; does not commit to either pole.
Foregrounds the state of being conditioned, the result or circumstance that has been established.
Explicitly frames these as objects that have been conditioned; the adjective makes the passive result-pole explicit.
Emphasizes ongoing activity, flowing and dynamic action; framed as active occurrences.
Foregrounds mental constructs as static things conceived rather than the act of conceiving.
Generic, colourless rendering that makes no commitment to active versus passive or result versus process.
Emphasizes the act of determining and deciding; foregrounds volitional activity and agency.
Explicitly names doing and action; clearly process-oriented and emphasizes ongoing enactment.
Ambiguous between the act of constructing and the thing constructed; does not commit to either pole.
Names the force of willing and volitional act; explicitly foregrounds the active, willing dimension.
Emphasizes active power, energy, and causal efficacy; foregrounds dynamism and the act of forcing.
Foregrounds that which is made up of parts; emphasizes the structured, composed result.
